predictions.text: By 2026-04-18, NASA will publicly confirm that Artemis II completed its planned mission and that the crewed Orion capsule splashed down safely with all crew recovered.
predictions.verification_criteria: NASA (or U.S. government partner agency) publishes an official mission-completion statement/press release confirming splashdown and crew recovery for Artemis II on or before 2026-04-18.
evidence: Structured summary notes Artemis II has launched, implying near-term mission completion and recovery milestones.
Recent NASA crewed missions typically publish same-day recovery confirmations via NASA.gov and social channels.
monitoring_signals: NASA Artemis blog updates and NASA.gov press releases
NASA TV mission coverage schedule and post-splashdown briefings
DoD recovery assets/public affairs updates (if involved)
predictions.rationale: Given the reported launch, the main remaining uncertainty is an in-mission anomaly; NASA usually confirms splashdown and crew recovery promptly if nominal.

predictions.text: By 2026-06-30, the EIA daily Europe Brent spot price (series DCOILBRENTEU) will be at or above $110 per barrel on at least one day.
predictions.verification_criteria: EIA series DCOILBRENTEU shows a daily observation >= 110.00 (USD/bbl) dated on or before 2026-06-30.
evidence: Structured summary highlights Hormuz-related risk and war-driven energy cost pass-through (Amazon surcharge; airline fees).
Chokepoint geopolitics historically increases crude risk premia and price volatility.
monitoring_signals: EIA DCOILBRENTEU daily updates
OPEC+ meeting statements and compliance estimates
Tankers/insurance war-risk pricing and transit disruptions tied to Hormuz
predictions.rationale: Prices are sensitive to any renewed shipping disruption; threshold is high but reachable under a risk-premium spike.

predictions.text: By 2026-06-30, at least one additional major U.S. marketplace/e-commerce platform (eBay, Etsy, Shopify, Walmart Marketplace, or Target Plus) will announce a temporary seller fee surcharge of at least 2.0% explicitly citing fuel and/or logistics costs.
predictions.verification_criteria: An official platform communication (press release, seller update, policy page, or investor filing) states a time-bounded surcharge/fee increase >=2.0% applied to sellers and explicitly attributes it to fuel/logistics/energy costs, dated on or before 2026-06-30.
evidence: Structured summary reports Amazon added a 3.5% seller fuel/logistics surcharge amid elevated fuel costs.
Energy shocks often lead to near-term surcharges rather than permanent fee resets.
monitoring_signals: Seller newsletters/policy change logs for the named platforms
Earnings call transcripts referencing 'fuel surcharge' or 'energy surcharge'
Freight indices and jet/diesel price moves that trigger surcharges
predictions.rationale: Amazon’s move can create competitive permission for similar pass-through, but platforms may resist to protect seller growth.

predictions.text: By 2026-07-31, Freddie Mac’s Primary Mortgage Market Survey (PMMS) will report the 30-year fixed-rate mortgage average at or above 7.50% in at least one weekly release.
predictions.verification_criteria: At least one weekly PMMS release dated on or before 2026-07-31 lists the 30-year fixed-rate mortgage average >= 7.50%.
evidence: Structured summary notes mortgage rates rising for a fifth straight week amid an energy-driven inflation shock.
Rate volatility tends to rise alongside inflation expectations and oil-price uncertainty.
monitoring_signals: Weekly Freddie Mac PMMS releases
10-year Treasury yields and inflation breakevens
CPI/PCE surprises and energy price moves
predictions.rationale: A further rate spike is plausible if inflation expectations stay elevated, but growth/financial-stress dynamics could cap yields.

predictions.text: By 2026-08-31, at least one major U.S. pharma industry group (PhRMA, the Association for Accessible Medicines, or BIO) will file a lawsuit in U.S. federal court challenging the legality of the newly announced U.S. pharmaceutical tariffs.
predictions.verification_criteria: A federal court complaint appears on PACER (or is publicly posted by the plaintiff) naming one of the listed groups as a plaintiff and challenging the pharma tariffs, filed on or before 2026-08-31.
evidence: Structured summary reports up to 100% tariffs on some imported drugs with many exemptions, implying high stakes and contested scope.
Trade measures with large sector impacts frequently prompt industry litigation.
monitoring_signals: PACER searches and plaintiff press releases
USTR/Federal Register implementation notices and tariff codes covered
Public statements from PhRMA/AAM/BIO about legal options
predictions.rationale: Industry may prefer negotiation, but large tariff levels increase incentives to litigate, especially if exemptions are narrow or unclear.

predictions.text: By 2026-09-30, at least one major U.S. retail private-credit fund (interval fund or tender-offer fund) managed by Blackstone, Apollo, Ares, KKR, or Carlyle will limit redemptions/tenders so that investors receive no more than 50% of the amount requested in a single offer period.
predictions.verification_criteria: A fund notice, tender-offer results announcement, shareholder letter, or SEC filing for an eligible fund states that proration/limits were applied and that accepted repurchases were <=50% of requests for that offer period, dated on or before 2026-09-30.
evidence: Structured summary reports Blue Owl capped private credit fund redemptions at 5% after heavy requests, highlighting liquidity mismatch stress.
Similar products share structural liquidity constraints that can propagate gating behavior.
monitoring_signals: SEC filings (N-CSR, N-PORT, tender-offer docs) and fund websites for repurchase results
Press coverage of 'proration' and 'repurchase offer' outcomes
Credit spreads and default/distress indicators that trigger withdrawal demand
predictions.rationale: Stress can spread through retail private credit, but large managers may use credit lines/cash buffers to avoid severe proration.

predictions.text: By 2026-10-31, Tesla will report automotive gross margin excluding regulatory credits at or below 15.0% in at least one quarterly earnings release (10-Q/10-K and shareholder deck) filed on or before that date.
predictions.verification_criteria: Tesla’s quarterly reporting (earnings release/shareholder deck and/or 10-Q/10-K) explicitly reports automotive gross margin ex regulatory credits <=15.0% for the quarter, with the filing/release dated on or before 2026-10-31.
evidence: Structured summary cites steep 2026 drop after weak deliveries, continued price cuts, and demand pressure—typically margin-negative.
Competitive EV pricing and incentives often compress margins during volume slowdowns.
monitoring_signals: Tesla quarterly earnings materials and 10-Q/10-K filings
Vehicle ASP trends, incentive levels, and inventory days
Battery/raw-material cost trends and China/US pricing actions
predictions.rationale: Weak demand plus continued discounting makes a <=15% quarter plausible, though cost-downs or mix shift could offset.

predictions.text: By 2026-12-31, China’s government will announce new export licensing restrictions or controls on at least one additional critical mineral/material (not already restricted as of 2026-03-31) that is used in semiconductors, batteries, or defense manufacturing.
predictions.verification_criteria: A PRC government body (e.g., MOFCOM) publishes an official notice adding export controls/licensing to at least one newly covered mineral/material category (new relative to 2026-03-31), on or before 2026-12-31.
evidence: Structured summary notes rival states leveraging strategic choke points (minerals/data routes/shipping lanes) in retaliation dynamics.
Recent years show iterative tightening of controls in response to sanctions/tariffs.
monitoring_signals: MOFCOM export control announcements and catalog updates
Chinese state media commentary on 'countermeasures' and supply-chain security
Industry alerts from semiconductor/battery supply-chain groups
predictions.rationale: Chokepoint retaliation is a common tool, but China may calibrate to avoid accelerating reshoring and substitution.

predictions.text: By 2026-12-31, OpenAI will publish at least 20 episodes of a recurring podcast or video show under an OpenAI or TBPN brand (counting only full episodes, excluding short clips).
predictions.verification_criteria: An OpenAI- or TBPN-branded show has >=20 distinct episode entries publicly available (RSS feed, YouTube playlist, or major podcast platform listing) with publication dates on or before 2026-12-31.
evidence: Structured summary notes OpenAI acquired TBPN to shape AI narrative, implying intent to scale media output.
Corporate media acquisitions typically target steady programming rather than one-off specials.
monitoring_signals: OpenAI/TBPN announcements and show feeds on major platforms
Hiring for producers/editors/hosts and content calendars
Cross-promotion in OpenAI product updates and newsletters
predictions.rationale: If OpenAI prioritizes trust and distribution, producing 20 episodes in nine months is operationally feasible; strategic shifts could slow cadence.
